headline: 18 regulatory agencies have never been sunset-reviewed since creation
context: Eighteen federal regulatory agencies established between 1970-2000 have never undergone a sunset review to assess whether their original mandate remains relevant or effective.

cards.big: 18 regulatory agencies created 25-55 years ago — never reviewed for continued relevance
cards.sub: Government bodies created in the Mahathir era continue unchanged in the 2020s

cards.big: No sunset clause exists in any of the 18 agencies' enabling legislation
cards.sub: Once created, regulatory agencies exist indefinitely without mandatory performance review

cards.big: 18 agencies cost combined RM4.8 billion annually in operational expenditure
cards.sub: Billions spent on agencies whose relevance has never been independently assessed

cards.big: Australia mandates regulatory reviews every 10 years under Productivity Commission framework
cards.sub: Systematic review ensures agencies evolve or are consolidated when no longer needed

cards.big: A regulatory agency that has never been reviewed is an assumption that has never been tested
Should all regulatory agencies face mandatory sunset review every 10 years?
